reasoning: Alabama is ranked No. 17 with a 22-7 record entering March 2026. They recently won three consecutive ranked/SEC games against #20 Arkansas, Mississippi State, and #22 Tennessee, proving they have high offensive capabilities but their defense has been leaky (e.g. giving up 100 to Florida and 115 to Arkansas). Betting odds from late February placed them as +6600 underdogs to win the National Championship, implying a raw probability of roughly 1-1.5% after removing the vigorish. Because Polymarket had previously priced this market at 1% and the current implied probability from bookmakers is closely aligned under 2%, the true probability of Alabama winning the 68-team tournament rests around 1.5-2%. I am selecting 0.02, the minimum acceptable value, safely reflecting their status as a compelling but definitive longshot compared to frontrunners with much stronger profiles.
